brief.query: Generate and validate a vtk.js scene whose prompt, code, and workflow input are all ready for corpus review vtk.js Browser Evidence Review Task The output should be suitable for prompt-sample-pro storage with stable prompt, retrieval context, starter code, and validation command.
recommendedPatterns: Expect screenshot, console log, page error log, and a structured render verdict.
Treat browser evidence as the acceptance contract for vtk.js scene validation.
Inject the vtk.js browser bundle before any module script that references global vtk.
Seed streamline scenes explicitly and confirm vector-field inputs before validation.
Build the scene in source -> mapper -> actor -> renderer order.
failureFixPairs: If artifacts are missing, rerun the canonical Docker workflow instead of improvising a different browser command.
When the page says vtk is not defined, restore the runtime script include before changing scene logic.
If streamline output is empty, inspect seed generation and pipeline ordering before general browser repair.
If validation fails early, retry with the minimal full-screen bootstrap scene first.
If the canvas is missing, reduce the page to a known-good bootstrap scene and compare new artifacts.
Capture artifact expectations per benchmark case so later eval output stays comparable.
brief.generationPrompt: Generate vtk.js code for the requested scene while preserving a deterministic browser-validation path. Scene kind: generic. Use these retrieval-backed patterns: Expect screenshot, console log, page error log, and a structured render verdict. | Treat browser evidence as the acceptance contract for vtk.js scene validation. | Inject the vtk.js browser bundle before any module script that references global vtk. | Seed streamline scenes explicitly and confirm vector-field inputs before validation. | Build the scene in source -> mapper -> actor -> renderer order. | Always call renderer.resetCamera() before renderWindow.render().. Avoid regressions captured by these failure-fix hints: If artifacts are missing, rerun the canonical Docker workflow instead of improvising a different browser command. | When the page says vtk is not defined, restore the runtime script include before changing scene logic. | If streamline output is empty, inspect seed generation and pipeline ordering before general browser repair. | If validation fails early, retry with the minimal full-screen bootstrap scene first. | If the canvas is missing, reduce the page to a known-good bootstrap scene and compare new artifacts. | Capture artifact expectations per benchmark case so later eval output stays comparable.. Acceptance contract: The scene should create a visible canvas during browser verification. | The script should end with renderer.resetCamera() followed by renderWindow.render(). | Browser evidence should include render-verification.json, browser-console.json, page-errors.json, and render-screenshot.png. | The browser console and page error logs should stay free of runtime errors..
acceptanceChecks: The scene should create a visible canvas during browser verification.
The script should end with renderer.resetCamera() followed by renderWindow.render().
Browser evidence should include render-verification.json, browser-console.json, page-errors.json, and render-screenshot.png.
The browser console and page error logs should stay free of runtime errors.

generatedCode.summary: vtk.js generated candidate for generic scene

starterNotes: This generated candidate keeps a deterministic browser-validation path by default.
The browser script is ready for Phase 5 render verification without additional bundling steps.
The scene should create a visible canvas during browser verification.
The script should end with renderer.resetCamera() followed by renderWindow.render().
Browser evidence should include render-verification.json, browser-console.json, page-errors.json, and render-screenshot.png.
